FT_IC1.evidence: Cross-sectional observational study from the first annual curated release of the U.S. Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study; 4524 children ages 8-11

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis with statistical modeling of associations between movement behavior recommendations and impulsivity dimensions

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 finds no phone/SM-specific variable with isolable coefficient. Step 2: composite includes sleep (9-11 h/night), recreational screen time (≤2 h/day), and physical activity (≥60 min/day) bundled together. Composite is multi-behavior (sleep + ST + PA), not exclusively phone/SM items; no per-device or per-platform breakout provided

notes: FT-IC2 fails because the screen time variable is part of a multi-behavior composite (24-hour movement) without isolated phone/SM reporting

FT_IC1.evidence: Cross-sectional study of 3102 children from the Amsterdam Born Children and their Development (ABCD) study in the Netherlands. This is the Dutch ABCD, not the U.S. NIH-funded Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study

notes: FT_IC1 NOT_MET: Dutch ABCD, not U.S. ABCD. Per user instruction, paper 116 marked as NOT_MET on FT_IC1

FT_IC1.evidence: Cross-sectional data from the first cross-sectional data release of the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study; 4277 participants with usable structural imaging and SMA data

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis using Group Factor Analysis (GFA) and generalized linear mixed-effect models to investigate associations between SMA and brain structure

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1: SMA is measured and analyzed as a qualifying composite variable. Methods state SMA includes: watching television or videos, playing video games, using social media. Paired with individual-trait-level neural variables (cortical thickness, sulcal depth, gray matter volume, brain structure indices). Other side meets individual-trait criterion (neural/brain structure)

FT_IC3.evidence: Multiple quantitative results reported: GFA coefficients with 95% credible intervals; Cohen's d effect sizes (0.04-0.1); model-level metrics (AIC, RMSEA); standardized coefficients for associations between SMA-related GFAs and psychopathology/cognition

FT_IC4.evidence: Numeric quantitative results throughout: GFA-derived standardized coefficients, effect sizes, AIC values, model comparisons with Chi-square statistics, Cohen's d estimates for externalizing behavior (ES 0.06-0.1) and intelligence (ES 0.04-0.1)

FT_EC7_reason: Cross-sectional design; FT-EC7 only applies to longitudinal designs
notes: Paper qualifies at full inclusion. SMA composite (TV, videos, video games, social media) meets Step 1 criterion when paired with neural outcomes
